titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Daniel Pinello Interviews on Same-sex Marriage
titlestmt.author: Camila Zorrilla Tessler

controlnote.p: This collection contains 95 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 3, 2024 at 08:00

did.unittitle: Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage
did.abstract: The Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage consist of oral histories (recordings and transcripts) that primarily focus on two series of events: First, the municipal issuance of marriage licenses in 2004 to lesbian and gay pairs in San Francisco, California, Sandoval County, New Mexico, and Multnomah County (Portland), Oregon, together with the statewide grant of such permits in Massachusetts. Second, the effects of Super-DOMAs (Defense of Marriage Acts) in Georgia, Michigan, North Carolina, Ohio, Texas, and Wisconsin. Super-DOMAs prohibited all forms of relationship recognition for same-sex couples and were ratified in 20 American states between 2000 and 2012. They were overturned when same-sex marriage was federally legalized in 2015.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Daniel R. Pinello, 2015 and 2023.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open to research unless otherwise noted.
Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Daniel R. Pinello graduated from Williams College with a B.A. in 1972, obtained a law degree from New York University in 1975 and a Ph.D. in political science from Yale University in 1991. He is a professor at John Jay College of Criminal Justice of the City University of New York and has written extensively on gay rights and the law.

The interviews in this collection stem from that focus, dealing especially with the impact on same-sex couples of Super-DOMA laws (Defense of Marriage Acts), or legislation that denied all rights of marriage to same-sex couples that existed in specific states previous to the federal right to same-sex marriage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage consist of oral histories (recordings and transcripts) that primarily focus on two series of events: First, the municipal issuance of marriage licenses in 2004 to lesbian and gay pairs in San Francisco, California, Sandoval County, New Mexico, and Multnomah County (Portland), Oregon, together with the statewide grant of such permits in Massachusetts. Second, the effects of Super-DOMAs (Defense of Marriage Acts) in Georgia, Michigan, North Carolina, Ohio, Texas, and Wisconsin. Super-DOMAs prohibited all forms of relationship recognition for same-sex couples and were ratified in 20 American states between 2000 and 2012. They were overturned when same-sex marriage was federally legalized in 2015. These interviews were conducted by Daniel Pinello, a professor at John Jay College of Criminal Justice of the City University of New York, between 2004 and 2013. Pinello carried out the videotaped conversations on location in the ten states as part of his research for two books: "America's Struggle for Same-Sex Marriage," published in 2006, and "America's War on Same-Sex Couples and their Families, And How the Courts Rescued Them," released in 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage collection is arranged chronologically.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The entire collection is available in digital form through the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
